2606.08806.title: Governance Controls for AI-Generated Test Artifacts in Autonomous Software Testing
2606.08806.paper_summary: Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly used in autonomous software testing; however, AI-generated test artifacts often suffer from hallucinations, compliance violations, security risks, and limited explainability. To enhance the reliability, transparency, and trustworthiness of AI-generated testing artifacts, this research introduces the concept of Governance-Aware Autonomous Testing Framework (GATF). The framework extends the autonomous testing lifecycle with governance validation, explainability analysis, probabilistic risk assessment, compliance monitoring, as well as audit governance. Experiments were performed with Defects4J and PROMISE software engineering datasets. The proposed framework successfully reduced the governance-related risks by 89.6% and demonstrated 94.3% accuracy in governance, 96.5% artifact reliability, 94.2% compliance accuracy, and 90.8% explainability performance. The results show that autonomous testing systems that are governance-aware can significantly enhance the reliability, transparency, and operational security of autonomous testing systems in comparison to conventional AI-based testing systems. The proposed architecture is scalable and reliable and provides a safe environment for software testing.
2606.08806.paper_summary_zh: 人工智慧（AI）和大型語言模型（LLMs）在自主軟體測試中的使用越來越普遍；然而，AI生成的測試產物常常會出現幻覺、合規違規、安全風險以及有限的可解釋性。為了增強AI生成測試產物的可靠性、透明度和可信度，本研究引入了治理感知自主測試框架（GATF）的概念。該框架擴展了自主測試生命周期，加入了治理驗證、可解釋性分析、概率風險評估、合規監控以及審計治理。實驗使用了Defects4J和PROMISE軟體工程數據集進行。所提出的框架成功地將與治理相關的風險降低了89.6%，並在治理上顯示出94.3%的準確率、96.5%的產物可靠性、94.2%的合規準確率以及90.8%的可解釋性表現。結果顯示，具有治理感知的自主測試系統相比於傳統的基於AI的測試系統，能顯著提升自主測試系統的可靠性、透明度和操作安全性。所提出的架構是可擴展且可靠的，並為軟體測試提供了一個安全的環境。
2606.08806.author: Dimple Bajaj et.al.
